originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'irq' during its initialization is never read

presenceCondition:
(or (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE (not CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN) CONFIG_OF_IRQ)
    (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN (not CONFIG_OF_IRQ))
    (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN CONFIG_OF_IRQ)
    (and (not CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE) CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN CONFIG_OF_IRQ)
    (and (not CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE) CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN (not CONFIG_OF_IRQ))
    (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE (not CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN) (not CONFIG_OF_IRQ)))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF_IRQ () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 6252547b8a7.c L12: irq dead store. 'int irq = *ops' assigns irq but irq is never subsequently used (function body ends at L13). Real condition: CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN=T (for irq_domain_add to exist, inside #ifdef CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN). PC covers many TWL4030/IRQ_DOMAIN/OF_IRQ combinations including contradictory configs: TWL4030_CORE=T AND !CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN is impossible (TWL4030_CORE=T is a derived macro that defines CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN, L3-5 of source). PC over-constrained by including non-compilable contradictory configurations. New bug.
